title: Lemna abbreviata
intro: Lemna abbreviata, also known as dwarf duckweed or pygmy duckweed, is a small aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Lemnaceae. Traditionally, Lemna abbreviata has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lemna abbreviata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lemna abbreviata is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, cough, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Lemna abbreviata is a species of duckweed, a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Lemnaceae. This family is a part of the order Alismatales and the class Liliopsida. The order Alismatales is a group of flowering plants that also includes water plantains and shoals grass. The class Liliopsida, also known as monocots, is a group of flowering plants that have one cotyledon in their seeds. Lemna abbreviata is a small, floating plant that grows in shallow water.
